# Medicare Moms

> Medicare Moms is an independent, licensed insurance agency helping families find the right Medicare, Medigap, ACA Marketplace, supplemental, and group health coverage. Licensed in 40+ states and Puerto Rico. Agents speak English, Spanish, Japanese, and German. A Wildcat Insurance company.

Medicare Moms is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to the federal Medicare program, CMS, or any government agency. Services are free to consumers; agents are paid by the insurance carriers when a plan is enrolled.

## What Makes Medicare Moms Different

Most insurance agencies are either captive (representing only one carrier), transactional (sell you a plan and disappear), or limited to Medicare alone. Medicare Moms is built differently:

- **Independent, not captive.** We work with 40+ insurance carriers, so our recommendation is based on what actually fits your doctors, medications, and budget — not on which carrier we're forced to sell. If a plan from a competitor's carrier is better for you, we tell you.
- **Full-spectrum insurance agency, not just Medicare.** We handle Medicare Advantage, Medicare Supplements (Medigap), Part D prescription plans, ACA Marketplace, private and underwritten individual health, dental, vision, life insurance, long-term care, accident & critical illness, and small and large group employer plans. One point of contact for an entire family's coverage across life stages.
- **Multilingual in-house.** Five of our licensed agents are bilingual English/Spanish (Aidyl Semidey, Elizabeth Perez, Edmi Dominguez, Lissette Wong, Magarely Sanchez). We also have a Japanese-speaking senior advisor (Saya Murton) and a German-speaking agent (Michelle Bodin). Clients speak with a licensed agent in their preferred language, not a translator.
- **Personalized, never cookie-cutter.** Every recommendation starts with a real conversation about the specific doctors you want to keep, the medications you take, your travel patterns, and your budget. We do not push one plan for every client. Most enrollments take 30–45 minutes precisely because we compare plans against your personal situation.
- **Year-round support.** Most agencies disappear after enrollment. We review client plans annually during Annual Enrollment, help with mid-year plan changes when a Special Enrollment Period opens, assist with appeals, and answer prescription or provider network questions whenever they come up.
- **Women-led, mom-to-mom approach.** Our agents understand families, caregiving decisions, and the pressure of helping aging parents. Clients often tell us it's the first time they've talked to an insurance professional who actually listens.
- **Licensed insurance experience going back to 1993, with a combined 100+ years across our team. Over 5,000 families served.** Not a call center, not a lead-generation funnel — a real agency with licensed professionals who specialize in these products.
- **Completely free to consumers.** Licensed insurance agents are paid directly by insurance carriers as commissions when a plan enrolls. You pay the same premium whether you enroll on your own or with us. There is no upsell, no consulting fee, no hidden cost.

## How We Work With Clients

1. **Listen first.** What doctors do you want to keep? What medications are you taking? Where do you live and travel? What's your budget? What matters to you — predictable costs, lowest premium, extras like dental?
2. **Research the market for your situation.** We compare plans across the 40+ carriers we represent in your specific zip code, against your specific drug list and provider list. We check formulary tiers, pharmacy networks, prior authorization requirements, and Star Ratings.
3. **Explain trade-offs in plain English.** Every plan has compromises. We walk through the real differences — not the marketing copy — and let you make the decision.
4. **Enroll only when you're ready.** No pressure. If you want to think about it overnight, that's fine. If you want a second opinion, we'll wait.
5. **Stay in touch year-round.** We contact you before each Annual Enrollment Period to review whether your plan still fits. We're available for questions any time your situation changes.

## Who Medicare Moms Serves

- **Seniors approaching 65 who need to understand Medicare for the first time.** We help sort through Part A, B, C, D, the difference between Medicare Advantage and Medigap, and the 7-month Initial Enrollment Period.
- **Current Medicare beneficiaries reviewing their plan.** If you're already on Medicare Advantage or a Part D plan, we can review whether a better option is available each Annual Enrollment Period.
- **People turning 65 who are still working.** Decisions around delaying Part B, coordinating employer coverage with Medicare, and avoiding late-enrollment penalties.
- **Individuals and families under 65 who need health insurance.** ACA Marketplace plans, private and underwritten plans, short-term options, and guidance around the 2026 subsidy changes after the enhanced tax credits expired.
- **Self-employed workers and small business owners.** Individual/family coverage and small group plans for businesses with 2+ employees.
- **Spanish-speaking families.** Full bilingual support in English and Spanish from five dedicated agents. Our Spanish-language site is at medicaremoms.com/es/.
- **Japanese and German speakers** needing health insurance and Medicare guidance in their language.
- **People with high-cost prescriptions** who benefit from the new $2,000 Part D out-of-pocket cap and who want a Part D plan optimized for their specific medications.
- **Anyone confused by the 2026 ACA subsidy changes.** The enhanced subsidies from the Inflation Reduction Act expired December 31, 2025. We help people understand how the changes affect them and whether they should stay on a Marketplace plan, shop off-Marketplace, check Medicaid eligibility, or combine coverage.

## Topics We Specialize In

- Turning 65 and Medicare Initial Enrollment Period decisions
- Medicare Advantage vs. Medigap plan comparisons
- Part D prescription plan optimization using the $2,000 annual out-of-pocket cap
- IRMAA surcharge planning and appealing IRMAA after a life change (retirement, divorce, death of spouse) using Form SSA-44
- Transitioning from employer coverage to Medicare
- Navigating the 2026 ACA Marketplace landscape after the expiration of the Inflation Reduction Act's enhanced premium tax credits
- ACA subsidy cliff at 400% FPL (returned for coverage year 2026)
- Cost-Sharing Reduction Silver plans (73%, 87%, and 94% AV tiers)
- Medicaid and CHIP eligibility and the Medicaid coverage gap in non-expansion states
- Special Enrollment Periods for life events (marriage, birth, job loss, moving, loss of coverage)
- Group health insurance for small and large employers
- Final expense and term life insurance
- Long-Term Care (LTC) planning

## Coverage Types We Offer

- [All Services](https://medicaremoms.com/services/): Full list of insurance products
- [Medicare Advantage (Part C)](https://medicaremoms.com/services/#medicare-advantage): All-in-one plans from all major carriers; many $0 premium options
- [Medicare Supplements / Medigap](https://medicaremoms.com/services/#medigap): Plans G, N, and others from multiple carriers for predictable cost and national provider freedom
- [Medicare Part D prescription plans](https://medicaremoms.com/services/#medicare-advantage): Drug plan shopping based on your actual medications
- [ACA / Marketplace plans](https://medicaremoms.com/services/#aca): Individual and family coverage under 65 with subsidy eligibility analysis
- [Private and underwritten health insurance](https://medicaremoms.com/services/#aca): Off-Marketplace options for people who don't qualify for subsidies or want alternatives
- [Dental, Vision & Supplemental](https://medicaremoms.com/services/#dental-vision): Standalone or bundled dental, vision, accident, and critical illness
- [Life Insurance & Long-Term Care](https://medicaremoms.com/services/#life-insurance): Term life, final expense, and LTC coverage
- Small and large group employer health plans for businesses

## Why Businesses and Agents Partner With Us

For licensed insurance agents joining our team:

- Not captive — sell what fits the client across 40+ carriers
- Multiple compensation paths: commission-only, base plus commission, commission split where we provide leads, and salaried positions
- Fully paid CRM, dedicated business phone number, business tracking software
- No quotas, no cold calling, no unfruitful prospecting activities
- Flexible schedule — most of our agents are moms who work around their family's time
- Ability to split business and build referral partnerships easily
- Part-time friendly path designed to let new agents learn before scaling up

See [Join Our Team](https://medicaremoms.com/join-our-team/) for details.

## Educational Resources

- [Medicare &amp; Insurance FAQ](https://medicaremoms.com/resources/medicare-faq/): 32 answered questions covering Medicare basics, Medicare Advantage, Medigap, Part D, ACA, Medicaid/CHIP, and working with an agent
- [Medicare Resources Hub](https://medicaremoms.com/resources/): Consumer guidance on Medicare
- [ACA & Individual Insurance Resources](https://medicaremoms.com/aca-resources/): Marketplace guidance
- [How to Enroll in Medicare](https://medicaremoms.com/enroll-medicare/): IEP, SEP, AEP, OEP, GEP, IRMAA guide
- [2026 ACA & Subsidy Cheat Sheet](https://medicaremoms.com/resources/aca-cheat-sheet-2026/): Full reference — FPL chart for households 1–8, subsidies, Medicaid/CHIP, MOOP, employer affordability, APTC repayment
- [2026 ACA Quick Reference (1-page)](https://medicaremoms.com/resources/aca-quickref-2026/): Printable agent/consumer cheat sheet

## Blog &amp; Current Data

- [Blog](https://medicaremoms.com/blog/): Ongoing Medicare and insurance content
- [Medicare by the Numbers 2026](https://medicaremoms.com/blog/medicare-statistics-2026/): Enrollment, costs, Inflation Reduction Act changes, One Big Beautiful Bill Act impact; sourced from CMS, KFF, CBO
- [2026 ACA Marketplace by the Numbers](https://medicaremoms.com/blog/aca-marketplace-2026/): Enrollment trends, enhanced subsidy expiration, OBBBA provisions, 2025 FPL chart
- [Understanding Medicare in 2026](https://medicaremoms.com/blog/medicare-guide-2026/): Full guide to Parts A, B, C, D for new enrollees

## TPMO Disclosure

We do not offer every plan available in your area. Any information we provide is limited to those plans we do offer in your area. Please contact Medicare.gov or 1-800-MEDICARE to get information on all of your options.
